rawCommits.subject: fix(sidekick): shrink stat value so time fits on one line
rawCommits.body:
Lower the sidekick StatCard value from 18px to 15px and add
white-space: nowrap so compound values like "13m 53s" no longer
wrap in the narrow sidekick column. Mobile variant still overrides
to 20px via .mobileStatValue.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: shrink stat value so time fits on one line

rawCommits.subject: fix(run-panel): persist structured task output across remounts and reloads
rawCommits.body:
Expanding a completed task row in the Run panel or the sidekick task
overlay was showing an empty body when the in-memory stream-store
entry had been pruned (5 min idle TTL / 40 entry cap) or the page had
been reloaded. The existing task-output-cache only persisted the raw
TextDelta stream, so rehydration lost the timeline, tool cards, and
thinking steps.

- Add task-turn-cache (localStorage) to persist the structured
  DisplaySessionEvent[] for each finalized task, with image-strip
  and result-truncation budgets so entries stay within quota.
- Snapshot the stream-store events into the turn cache on
  TaskCompleted, TaskFailed, and loop-end transitions.
- Protect recently-finalized stream entries from the pruning TTL so
  in-session collapse/expand keeps rendering from live memory, and
  add a seedStreamEventsFromCache helper for cold rehydration.
- Introduce useTaskOutputView, a unified hook that walks
  live stream then turn cache then event store then server hydration
  in order, used by both CompletedTaskOutput and TaskOutputSection.
- Invalidate the turn cache on task restart, dismiss, and
  clear-completed so stale runs do not render against new attempts.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: persist structured task output across remounts and reloads

rawCommits.subject: vendor(chromiumoxide): fork v0.9.1 to diagnose WS Invalid message flood
rawCommits.body:
Vendor the full chromiumoxide + subcrates (v0.9.1 from
mattsse/chromiumoxide@a7e2bb8) under vendor/chromiumoxide/ and pin the
workspace at that fork via [patch.crates-io], excluding the vendor
tree from our outer workspace so its own [workspace] remains
self-contained.

The browser backend was emitting a continuous stream of
`WARN chromiumoxide::handler: WS Invalid message: data did not match
any variant of untagged enum Message` during every CDP session. 0.9.1
already includes upstream PR #246 which intentionally ignores these
serde failures at WARN level, but the original log line only prints
the serde error and drops the raw JSON payload, so there is no way to
tell which CDP event or response schema actually drifted.

The only local edit in this commit is in
vendor/chromiumoxide/src/handler/mod.rs: destructure
`CdpError::InvalidMessage(payload, serde_err)` and include `payload`
as a tracing field on the WARN. With that, the next run identifies
each failing `method` by name and we can add targeted
`#[serde(rename = "...")]` / `#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]`
patches to the affected generated structs in chromiumoxide_cdp in
follow-up commits, then upstream them.

No application-side code changes; crates/aura-os-browser builds
unchanged against the patched crate (cargo check --workspace
--features aura-os-browser/cdp).

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: vendor(chromiumoxide): fork v0.9.1 to diagnose WS Invalid message flood

rawCommits.subject: refactor(run-panel): remove unused bottom TaskOutputPanel surface
rawCommits.body:
The resizable, collapsible `TaskOutputPanel` that used to live at the
bottom of the project layout was replaced some time ago by the Sidekick
drawer's `RunSidekickPane` and `TerminalSidekickPane`. The old export
had no remaining call sites in app code (only its own test file still
imported it), so the resize handle, collapse chevron, run/terminal tab
switcher, and their persisted `panelHeight` / `collapsed` / `activeTab`
state were dead weight.

- Delete the `TaskOutputPanel` component, its `PanelTabs` helper, and
  the back-compat `useActiveTaskTracking` no-op hook.
- Drop the corresponding exports from `components/TaskOutputPanel/index.ts`
  and the now-stale mock entry from `Sidekick.test.tsx`.
- Strip `panelHeight`, `collapsed`, `activeTab`, `toggleCollapse`,
  `setActiveTab`, and `handleMouseDown` from `useTaskOutputPanelStore`,
  along with the `aura-task-output-panel` localStorage key that only
  those fields used. Tasks persistence is unaffected. A one-shot
  `removeItem` for the old key tidies existing client storage.
- Remove the now-unused `.panel`, `.panelCollapsed`, `.resizeHandle`,
  `.header`, `.headerLeft`, `.headerTabs`, `.panelTabsRoot`,
  `.panelTabBtn`, `.tabDot*`, `.addTerminalBtn`, `.headerLabel`, and
  `.wifiIcon` rules from `TaskOutputPanel.module.css`.
- Update the store tests to drop assertions about the deleted fields.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: fix(sidekick-stats): tint Active card green and update it live
rawCommits.body:
The Active stat card now uses the app's standard success green
(var(--color-success)) via a new accent="success" StatCard prop so it
matches the active indicators used across TaskOutputPanel, ProcessList,
and VmStatusBadge.

The stats dashboard also subscribes to TaskStarted/TaskCompleted/
TaskFailed events scoped to the current project, applying an optimistic
adjustment to in_progress_tasks/done_tasks/failed_tasks and a debounced
refetch so the grid tracks the automation loop in real time instead of
waiting for a chat stream to end.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: tint Active card green and update it live

rawCommits.subject: fix(loop): hydrate active task state via GET /loop/status on refresh
rawCommits.body:
After a page refresh the top sidekick spinner kept spinning because
`/loop/status` correctly reported `running=true` from the in-memory
automaton registry, but the Run panel and the TaskList "live" badge
went dark — both relied on one-shot `task_started` WebSocket events
that are never replayed on reconnect.

Expose the registry's per-agent "currently streaming" task id over
HTTP and teach the UI to seed the affected stores from it:

- `ActiveAutomaton` now tracks `current_task_id`; the dev-loop
  forwarder mirrors the task id on `task_started` (and clears it on
  `task_completed` / `task_failed`) so `/loop/status`, `/loop/start`,
  `/loop/pause`, `/loop/stop`, `/loop/resume` all surface
  `active_tasks: [{ task_id, agent_instance_id }]`.
- `useAutomationStatus` hydrates `useTaskOutputPanelStore` with
  `hydrateActiveTask(...)` placeholders whenever it polls the loop
  status, and `useProjectLayoutData`'s boot reconcile now also
  passes task titles so those placeholders get a real label once
  `listTasks` returns.
- `useTaskListData` polls `/loop/status` on mount and reconnect to
  seed `liveTaskIds`, keeping the per-task spinner in the Tasks tree
  in step with the nav spinner.
- `useLoopStatus` accepts a project id and hydrates the sidebar
  agent indicator from `/loop/status` on mount so the left-nav
  spinner no longer needs a fresh `LoopStarted` event to light up.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: hydrate active task state via GET /loop/status on refresh

rawCommits.subject: feat(sidekick): persist active tab across reloads
rawCommits.body:
Remember the last-active tab for each sidekick (main, agents, process)
in localStorage so the app no longer snaps back to the terminal/profile/
process tab after refresh. createSidekickSlice now accepts an optional
persistence config that hydrates the initial tab from storage and writes
on every setActiveTab; stores that override setActiveTab use a shared
persistActiveTab helper.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: chore(cargo): disable color and progress output workspace-wide
rawCommits.body:
Agent/CI tool transports Base64-wrap streams that contain ANSI escapes
and CR-based progress updates, producing unreadable blobs instead of
plain diagnostics. Setting `[term] color = "never"` and
`progress.when = "never"` in `.cargo/config.toml` gives cargo plain
line-buffered output everywhere in this workspace, keeping compiler
errors legible in automated runs. Humans can still opt back in per
invocation with `--color=always`.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: disable color and progress output workspace-wide

rawCommits.subject: feat(debug): persist dev-loop runs to disk and add Debug app
rawCommits.body:
Every dev-loop run is now written to a structured on-disk bundle
(metadata.json + events/llm_calls/iterations/blockers/retries jsonl +
summary.md) by LoopLogWriter, wired through dev_loop event forwarding
so capture is always on. A new /api/debug/* surface exposes projects,
runs, metadata, summary, per-channel logs, and a .zip export. The
interface gains a Debug app (Bug icon, after Integrations) with a
project/run nav, a run list view, and a run detail view featuring a
virtualized timeline, channel/type/text filters, JSON inspector, copy,
and zip export -- enabling analysis of LLM retries, blockers, and
iteration cost across aura-os runs.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: persist dev-loop runs to disk and add Debug app

rawCommits.subject: feat(process): persist process-node live output across reloads
rawCommits.body:
The Run preview's "Live Output" panel was driven entirely by an
in-memory stream store, so a page reload or WS reconnect during an
in-flight run would flash the panel empty until the next batch of WS
events arrived. Mirror the existing task turn-cache flow for process
runs: add `process-node-turn-cache` (localStorage) plus an app-scoped
`process-stream-bootstrap` that snapshots the live entries on node /
run completion, seed `useProcessNodeStream` from the cache on mount,
and persist `liveRunNodeId` per-run so the focused node rehydrates to
the correct run after a refresh. Also broadens the panel's render gate
to include cache-backed state, retitled "Recent Live Output" when the
run is no longer actively streaming.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: persist process-node live output across reloads

rawCommits.subject: feat(debug): associate runs with specs, add run-analyze CLI + heuristics
rawCommits.body:
- spec association: track the set of specs any task in a run touched
  via RunMetadata.spec_ids and RunTaskSummary.spec_id. dev_loop now
  resolves task -> spec via StorageClient::get_task before calling
  on_task_started. GET /api/debug/projects/:id/runs accepts an optional
  ?spec_id= filter. The Debug nav groups Project -> Spec -> Runs with
  depth-1 FolderSections, and the run list view pre-filters from a
  ?spec= query param with a clearable chip.

- schema crate: extract RunMetadata / RunCounters / RunTaskSummary /
  RunStatus + the classify_debug_file helper into a tiny new
  aura-loop-log-schema crate so both the server and the new CLI can
  share the on-disk format without a circular dep.

- heuristics crate: aura-run-heuristics implements seven rules --
  repeated_blocker_path, high_retry_density, slow_iteration,
  token_hog_llm_call, unbalanced_io, task_never_completed,
  zero_tool_calls_in_turn -- each with unit tests.

- aura-run-analyze CLI: --latest / --run / --list commands with
  markdown and json output; exits 1 on warnings, 2 on errors so CI can
  gate on it.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: associate runs with specs, add run-analyze CLI + heuristics

rawCommits.subject: fix(run): show current task instantly on Run, including re-runs
rawCommits.body:
Keep the Run button and sidekick Run/Tasks tab spinners engaged
continuously from click through the first task_started event, and seed
the Run panel row + Tasks-list "live" dot from the /loop/start response
so the first (or interrupted) task of a new run no longer looks idle
during the HTTP->WS handoff.

- Optimistically flip preparing=true in handleStart so the spinner
  doesn't flicker off between the HTTP response and the loop_started
  WS event; clear it only when task_started (or a terminal event)
  arrives.
- Extract live task ids into a shared zustand store so the Run-start
  path can hydrate them synchronously, and so the desktop Tasks list
  and mobile task views stay consistent.
- Hydrate both the Run panel (task_output_panel_store) and the
  live-task-ids store from the active_tasks already returned by
  /loop/start and /loop/resume (the backend resolves the first /
  interrupted task id before responding).

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: show current task instantly on Run, including re-runs

rendered.title: Run state that survives refresh, plus a new Debug app for dev-loop runs
rendered.intro: Today's nightly is heavy on durability: task and process-run output now rehydrate cleanly across reloads and WebSocket reconnects, the sidekick remembers where you were, and every dev-loop run is archived to disk behind a new Debug app and CLI. A vendored chromiumoxide fork unlocks deeper browser-backend debugging, and the release pipeline grew an automated screenshot media step.

entries.title: Task output survives reloads and stream pruning
entries.summary: The Run panel's completed task rows no longer flash empty after a refresh or idle timeout, and a small sidekick layout fix keeps compound timers on one line.

items.text: Completed task rows now rehydrate their full timeline, tool cards, and thinking steps from a new localStorage turn cache, with a unified useTaskOutputView hook that walks live stream → turn cache → event store → server hydration so expanding a row after a reload or 5-minute idle never shows a blank body.
Shrunk the sidekick StatCard value to 15px with nowrap so compound times like "13m 53s" stop wrapping in the narrow column, while the mobile variant keeps its larger size.

media.reason: Entry looks like a user-visible product change and should receive changelog media.
reasons: entry language points to a visible product surface
media.alt: Task output survives reloads and stream pruning screenshot

entries.title: Vendored chromiumoxide fork to trace CDP schema drift
entries.summary: The browser backend's noisy "WS Invalid message" warnings can finally be diagnosed: chromiumoxide 0.9.1 is now vendored in-tree with a local patch that logs the offending CDP payload.

items.text: Forked chromiumoxide 0.9.1 under vendor/chromiumoxide and pinned the workspace at it via [patch.crates-io], with a single local edit to the handler that attaches the raw JSON payload to the InvalidMessage warning — so each failing CDP method name is now visible and can be patched with targeted serde renames upstream.

media.reason: Entry does not look like a reliable screenshot target, so no media slot was requested.
media.alt: Vendored chromiumoxide fork to trace CDP schema drift screenshot

entries.title: Live run state rehydrates across refresh
entries.summary: Several fixes close the gap where refreshing during an active run left spinners, badges, and panels out of sync with the automaton loop, plus a live-updating Active stat and a persisted sidekick tab.

items.text: Refreshing during an active run now correctly lights up the Run panel, the TaskList live dot, and the left-nav agent spinner: /loop/status, /loop/start, /loop/pause, /loop/stop, and /loop/resume all surface the currently streaming task id, and the UI seeds its stores from it on mount and reconnect instead of waiting for one-shot task_started WebSocket events.

The sidekick Active stat card is now tinted with the standard success green and updates live via TaskStarted/TaskCompleted/TaskFailed subscriptions, so in-progress/done/failed counts track the automation loop in real time instead of waiting for a chat stream to end.
Each sidekick (main, agents, process) now remembers its last-active tab in localStorage, so the app no longer snaps back to terminal/profile/process after a refresh.

Retired the old bottom TaskOutputPanel surface that had been superseded by the Sidekick drawer, removing its resize handle, collapse state, tab switcher, and stale aura-task-output-panel localStorage key (with a one-shot cleanup for existing clients).

media.reason: Entry looks like a user-visible product change and should receive changelog media.
reasons: entry language points to a visible product surface
media.alt: Live run state rehydrates across refresh screenshot

entries.title: Debug app, run-analyze CLI, and persistent process Live Output
entries.summary: Every dev-loop run is now archived to disk and inspectable through a new in-app Debug surface and a Rust CLI with built-in heuristics; process runs gain the same reload-survives-refresh treatment as tasks.

items.text: Every dev-loop run is written to a structured on-disk bundle (metadata, events, LLM calls, iterations, blockers, retries, summary) via a new LoopLogWriter, exposed through /api/debug/* with project/run listings, per-channel logs, and a .zip export, and surfaced by a new Debug app featuring a virtualized run timeline, channel/type/text filters, a JSON inspector, and export.

Runs are now grouped by the specs their tasks touched (Project → Spec → Runs in the Debug nav, with a ?spec_id= filter), and a new aura-run-analyze CLI with seven heuristics — repeated blockers, high retry density, slow iteration, token-hog LLM calls, unbalanced I/O, never-completed tasks, zero-tool-call turns — emits markdown or JSON and exits non-zero so CI can gate on warnings or errors. Shared types live in a new aura-loop-log-schema crate.

The process Run preview's Live Output panel now persists across reloads and WS reconnects via a new process-node-turn-cache and process-stream-bootstrap, with the focused node rehydrating to the correct run and the section retitled "Recent Live Output" when the run is no longer actively streaming.

Clicking Run now keeps the button and sidekick Run/Tasks spinners engaged continuously from click through the first task_started event — including re-runs — by optimistically flipping preparing=true and seeding a shared live-task-ids store from the active_tasks returned by /loop/start and /loop/resume.

Disabled cargo color and progress output workspace-wide so agent/CI transports that Base64-wrap streams stop producing unreadable blobs of ANSI escapes and carriage-return progress updates in automated runs.

entry language points to release plumbing rather than a product screen

entries.title: Automated screenshot media for the release changelog
entries.summary: The release pipeline now captures demo screenshots as part of publishing the changelog, with a dedicated follow-up workflow and matching trigger wiring.

items.text: Added a Publish Release Changelog Media workflow plus a screenshot-first capture pipeline — Browserbase- or Playwright-backed — with a demo seed planner, agent brief and app catalog, screenshot quality checks, and an in-app capture bridge, all documented in docs/demo-screenshot-pipeline.md.
Realigned the publish-release-changelog workflow's triggers so the new media job runs on the right events without racing the changelog publish step.

highlights: Task output and sidekick stats survive reloads
New Debug app + aura-run-analyze CLI for dev-loop runs
Run spinners stay accurate through refresh and re-runs
Release changelog now auto-captures screenshot media
